title: Explain the roles of @Component, @Service, @Repository, and @Controller annotations in Spring.

answers.junior: These annotations mark classes for Spring to manage. @Component is general, @Service is for business logic, @Repository is for data access, and @Controller is for web endpoints.
answers.middle: @Component marks any bean, @Service is for business logic, @Repository handles data and exception translation, @Controller manages web requests. Using specific annotations clarifies intent and enables features like AOP.
answers.senior: @Component is generic, @Service is for business logic and AOP, @Repository adds exception translation for persistence, @Controller maps HTTP requests. Using stereotypes improves clarity and maintainability. All are detected via component scanning.
answers.tricky: @Repository enables exception translation for database errors. @Service allows AOP features. @Controller integrates with Spring MVC. All annotations result in bean registration, but their roles affect behavior and integration. Proper use supports separation of concerns and advanced Spring features.
